title: Request Authority, Host, Target Reconstruction, and Routing · Failure Repair Bay
summary: An incident engineer receives a plausible but defective authority validation and target reconstruction table; retain the raw transcript, isolate the first divergent state, and preserve a regression corpus entry. Planned artifact: Repair a changed failure in authority validation and target reconstruction table and publish the reproduction, evidence, repair, and regression record. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Require exactly one valid Host field for HTTP/1.1 where applicable and reject missing, empty, duplicate, or invalid authority.
Reconstruct the effective request URI from target form, request-target, Host, connection security, and trusted configuration.
Handle proxy absolute-form authority and Host according to RFC rules while rejecting unsafe disagreement.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: Inspect the failure record · Debug Host Field Requirement
steps.instruction:
debug case — A debug protocol team handles case 5227 in a partner TLS endpoint negotiating HTTP/1.1 and HTTP/2 through ALPN. Build deterministic Go evidence to derive and allowlist one effective request authority without trusting disagreement; preserve this operating constraint: one parser state transition may consume bytes only after its full precondition holds; then replace one field with repeated non-combinable lines and preserve their order. Browser code models bounded byte and state decisions only. Sockets, listeners, live TLS, proxies, packet capture, fuzz campaigns, races, load, HTTP/2, HTTP/3, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates. Competency probe: Require exactly one valid Host field for HTTP/1.1 where applicable and reject missing, empty, duplicate, or invalid authority.

Inspect the Host Field Requirement trace for this debug case. Which record connects a precondition, observed state change, and boundary result?

Evidence target: Inspect the failure record · Debug Host Field Requirement.

For Inspect the failure record · Debug Host Field Requirement, keep Host Field Requirement connected to executable Go behavior evidence.

content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Distinguish connection IP, TLS SNI, certificate identity, ALPN, HTTP Host or authority, and application tenant evidence.

hints: For Publish the incident record · Debug Tls Sni Host Boundary, name the types, ownership, and stop path, Tls Sni Host Boundary precondition, or invariant that must hold.

At Publish the incident record · Debug Tls Sni Host Boundary, trace this concrete input and predict values, errors, blocking, and output before reviewing it: A debug protocol team handles case 5235 in a fuzzing harness varying every legal input segmentation. Build deterministic Go evidence to derive and allowlist one effective request authority without trusting disagreement; preserve this operating constraint: one parser state transition may consume bytes only after its full precondition holds; then truncate one content octet and classify incomplete framing without connection reuse. Browser code models bounded byte and state decisions only. Sockets, listeners, live TLS, proxies, packet capture, fuzz campaigns, races, load, HTTP/2, HTTP/3, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates. Competency probe: Distinguish connection IP, TLS SNI, certificate identity, ALPN, HTTP Host or authority, and application tenant evidence.

For Publish the incident record · Debug Tls Sni Host Boundary, challenge the documented misconception with runtime, failure, race, or toolchain evidence: Matching SNI and Host proves the authenticated user and intended application tenant.
